_comment: Central config for all external data sources used by the ETL pipeline. Scripts in /etl should read from this file rather than hardcoding URLs, so a source change is a one-line edit here, not a code change.

mta_subway_hourly_ridership.description: Model 1 demand input, Model 3 crowding input
mta_subway_hourly_ridership.notes: Confirmed live and returning valid records. ridership/transfers fields return as strings - cast to numeric in ETL. Field is station ENTRIES, not journey legs - see project notes.

mta_subway_major_incidents.notes: CORRECTED during Phase 2 build: the originally configured id (uqnw-2qfk, 'Beginning 2025' edition) returns 403 'authentication_required' - it is not publicly accessible despite being catalogued as such. ereg-mcvp is the actual public, live edition of the same 'Major Incidents' series (data confirmed current through 2026-05), found via the Socrata catalog API (api.us.socrata.com/api/catalog/v1). Monthly granularity, by line/category/division/day_type (1=weekday, 2=weekend only - no finer day-of-week split exists in the public data). 'Major incident' = 50+ trains delayed by MTA's own definition - undercounts routine minor delays, disclosed per PRD FR-12/NG6. line values use 'JZ' (combined) and 'S 42nd'/'S Rock'/'S Fkln' (separate shuttles, unlike this project's combined 'S' label) - see lib/route_labels.py. The W train has zero rows in this dataset's entire history; its incidents are historically folded into 'N' reporting (confirmed via a broader sibling dataset's column description) - handled as a disclosed pass-through from N, not a fabricated zero.

mta_subway_customer_journey_metrics.description: Model 2 delay/incident basis - delay minutes (Additional Platform Time + Additional Train Time)
mta_subway_customer_journey_metrics.dataset_label: MTA Subway Customer Journey-Focused Metrics: Beginning 2015
mta_subway_customer_journey_metrics.notes: Found during Phase 2 build via the Socrata catalog API - not in the original source list. Monthly granularity, by line x peak/off-peak period (no weekday/weekend split at all, a different split than the incidents dataset above). additional_platform_time + additional_train_time = average extra minutes per rider, per MTA's own published methodology. Includes W (unlike the incidents datasets above).

gtfs_static_subway_regular.description: Fallback/baseline schedule - updated only a few times a year
gtfs_static_subway_regular.source: MTA official developer announcement (HTTPS migration), confirmed still active via third-party feed-health monitor

gtfs_static_subway_supplemented.description: Preferred supply-side source - reflects planned service changes, updated hourly
gtfs_static_subway_supplemented.notes: Use this over the regular feed for Model 1's scheduled-supply calculation.

nyc_special_event_permits.notes: Not yet verified - confirm exact dataset ID/endpoint before Phase 3 build starts.

us_holiday_calendar.notes: Recommended over hardcoding a date list - confirm package choice during Phase 1 implementation.
